About.title: About
About.lead: Baikal Guide is a modern digital platform for tourism and culture in Buryatia.
body: We cover routes, events, food and music along Baikal's eastern shore — for travellers aged 17–35.
The project promotes tourism, showcases Buryat culture and grows a young community around the region.
All content is bilingual and continuously updated.

Faq.title: FAQ
Faq.subtitle: The essentials for a trip to Baikal and Buryatia.

items.q: When is the best time to visit Baikal?
items.a: Summer for treks and water, winter for ice and photography. Each route lists its recommended season.

items.q: Do I need a permit for national parks?
items.a: Some protected areas require a permit. Check the description of each route.
The main gateway is Ulan-Ude: airport and railway. From there, take a transfer or rent a car.

Contacts.title: Contacts
Contacts.subtitle: Get in touch with the team.
Contacts.body: Have a route idea, an event for the calendar or a partnership proposal? Write to us.

GettingThere.title: Getting there
GettingThere.subtitle: The main gateway to Buryatia is Ulan-Ude. Baikal is a few hours away from there.

sections.title: By air
sections.body: Ulan-Ude Baikal International Airport (UUD) handles direct flights from Moscow, Novosibirsk, Irkutsk and other cities. From the airport, take a taxi (~20–30 minutes) or a city bus to the centre.

sections.title: By train
sections.body: The Trans-Siberian Railway runs through Ulan-Ude, connecting it with Moscow, Irkutsk, Vladivostok and Mongolia (toward Ulaanbaatar). The station is in the city centre.

sections.title: By car
sections.body: The R-258 “Baikal” federal highway links Irkutsk and Ulan-Ude (~450 km along the southern shore). Renting a car is convenient for independent trips around the republic.

sections.title: To the Baikal shore
sections.body: From Ulan-Ude to the eastern-shore villages (Gremyachinsk, Goryachinsk, Ust-Barguzin) there are scheduled buses, marshrutkas or transfers. Travel time ranges from 2 to 6 hours depending on the destination.

Transport.title: Transport
Transport.subtitle: How to get around Ulan-Ude and the republic.

sections.title: Around the city
sections.body: Ulan-Ude has buses and marshrutkas; pay the driver in cash or by card. Taxis are easiest to order through apps — fast and inexpensive.

sections.title: Between towns and villages
sections.body: Intercity buses and marshrutkas depart from the bus station. For popular destinations (Barguzin, Kurumkan, Kyakhta) it's best to buy tickets in advance during the season.

sections.title: Car rental
sections.body: Rentals are available in the city and at the airport. For long or mountain routes choose all-wheel drive; watch the forecast and keep fuel topped up — petrol stations are sparse in remote areas.

sections.title: Good to know
sections.body: Distances are large and some roads are unpaved. Plan extra time, keep cash on hand and download offline maps in advance — coverage isn't everywhere.

Safety.title: Safety
Safety.subtitle: Basic rules for a smooth trip.

sections.title: Nature and weather
sections.body: Weather on Baikal changes quickly even in summer: bring warm and waterproof clothing. Wear a life vest on the water, never step onto the ice without a guide in winter, and respect the lake's power.

sections.title: Health
sections.body: Get travel insurance. In remote areas medical help may be far away — carry a first-aid kit, tick and sun protection. If possible, get a tick-borne encephalitis vaccination in advance.

sections.title: Connectivity and navigation
sections.body: Mobile coverage is reliable in cities and weaker in the mountains and along the shore. Download offline maps, and tell someone your route and expected return time.

sections.title: Protected areas
sections.body: Some routes cross national parks — a permit and following the rules are required. Don't leave litter, don't light fires outside designated spots, and protect the fragile, protected wildlife.

sections.title: Emergency services
sections.body: The single emergency number is 112. Save it along with your guide's or tour operator's contacts before heading out.

Partners.title: Partners
Partners.subtitle: Who we build the project with and how to join.

sections.title: Who it's for
sections.body: We're open to working with tour operators, local guides, hotels and resorts, cafés and local producers, event organisers and content creators.

sections.title: What we offer
sections.body: Listing your routes and events, featuring your place in blog stories, and promotion to a young audience interested in Buryatia and Baikal.

sections.title: How to become a partner
sections.body: Write to us via the feedback form or by email — tell us about yourself and your idea. We'll get in touch to discuss the format.

Feedback.title: Feedback
Feedback.subtitle: A question, an idea or a partnership proposal — write to us and we'll reply.

Eco.title: Protect Baikal
Eco.subtitle: Baikal is a UNESCO World Heritage site and the deepest lake on Earth. Travel so it stays that way for everyone who comes after you.

sections.title: Why it matters
sections.body: Baikal holds about 20% of the world's unfrozen fresh water and hundreds of endemic species found nowhere else. The ecosystem is fragile: a cigarette butt, soap in the water, a shortcut off the trail — small things carry a real cost here. Responsible travel isn't a restriction; it's how we keep the place we came for.

sections.title: Leave no trace
sections.body: Pack out all your rubbish, including food scraps and cigarette butts. Don't use soap or shampoo in the lake or rivers. Stay on existing trails and don't cut corners — trampled slopes take decades to recover. Make fires only at designated spots and where permitted.

sections.title: Protected areas
sections.body: Many routes cross national parks (Zabaikalsky, Pribaikalsky) and nature reserves. These require a permit and registration, with rules for camping and fires. Check the status of an area in advance — each route's description notes where a permit is needed.

sections.title: Respect people and wildlife
sections.body: Buryatia is a living culture with Buddhist and shamanic traditions. At sacred sites (datsans, ovoo) behave modestly and ask permission before photographing people. Keep your distance from wildlife and the Baikal seal, and never feed them.

sections.title: How to help
sections.body: Choose local guides and small family-run places — the money stays in the region. Join volunteer shore clean-ups. Carry a reusable bottle and a rubbish bag. Tell friends about treating Baikal gently — leading by example works better than any rule.

adrenaline.title: For adrenaline
adrenaline.intro: Treks on the edge, summits and active routes for those chasing the rush and views you have to earn.

soul.title: For the soul
soul.intro: Quiet shores, datsans and slow routes where you can breathe out, reset and be alone with Baikal.

culture.title: For culture
culture.intro: Buddhist temples, traditions and the history of Buryatia — routes for those who want to understand the region, not just see it.

content.title: For content
content.intro: The most photogenic spots, views and textures of Baikal — routes that make the best shots and stories.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.subtitle: How we collect, use and protect your data when you use Baikal Guide. This document is a template and must be reviewed by a lawyer and completed with the operator's details before publication.

sections.title: 1. General
sections.body: This Policy explains what data the Baikal Guide website (the “Site”) and its operator — [Operator name] — process and how it is protected. By using the Site you agree to this Policy. If you do not agree, please do not use the Site.

sections.title: 2. What data we collect
sections.body:
Data you provide: your name and email when registering, sending feedback or subscribing to the newsletter; the text of your comments and route ratings.
Data collected automatically: IP address, device and browser type, language, the pages you open, and cookies.

sections.title: 3. Purposes
sections.body: We use data to: provide access to your account, favorites, comments and ratings; respond to your enquiries; send the newsletter (if you subscribed); improve the Site and analyse its use; and comply with legal requirements.

sections.title: 4. Legal bases
sections.body: We process data based on your consent, the need to provide you with the Site's services, our legitimate interests (security and improvement of the Site), and the requirements of applicable law.

sections.title: 5. Cookies and analytics
sections.body: The Site uses cookies to run the interface, remember your language choice and for analytics. You can disable cookies in your browser settings, but some features may not work correctly. Analytics uses anonymised statistics.

sections.title: 6. Sharing with third parties
sections.body: We do not sell your data. Data may be shared with service providers (hosting, analytics, mailing) acting on our behalf, and with authorised bodies where required by law.

sections.title: 7. Storage and protection
sections.body: Data is kept no longer than necessary for the stated purposes or required by law. We take reasonable organisational and technical measures to protect data from unauthorised access, alteration and loss.

sections.title: 8. Your rights
sections.body: You may request access to your data, its correction or deletion, withdraw consent to processing and the newsletter, and lodge a complaint with the competent authority. To do so, write to the address below.

sections.title: 9. Minors
sections.body: The Site is not intended for children under 14, and we do not knowingly collect their data. If you believe we have received such data, let us know and we will delete it.

sections.title: 10. Changes and contact
sections.body: We may update this Policy; the current version is always available on this page. For data questions, write to hello@baikal-guide.ru.

PersonalData.title: Personal Data Processing Policy
PersonalData.subtitle: A document under Russian Federal Law No. 152-FZ “On Personal Data”. This is a template — fill in the operator's details and have it reviewed by a lawyer before publication.

sections.title: 1. Operator
sections.body: Personal data operator: [Operator name], INN [INN], OGRN [OGRN], address: [address]. Contact for personal-data matters: hello@baikal-guide.ru.

sections.title: 2. Terms
sections.body: The terms “personal data”, “processing”, “data subject”, “operator” and others are used as defined by Russian Federal Law No. 152-FZ of 27 July 2006 “On Personal Data”.

sections.title: 3. Principles
sections.body: The operator processes personal data lawfully and fairly, for predefined and lawful purposes, not excessively relative to those purposes, ensuring accuracy and confidentiality of the data.

sections.title: 4. Subjects and data
sections.body: We process data of visitors and registered users of the Site: name/username, email address, the content of enquiries and comments, and technical data (IP address, device and browser information, cookies).

sections.title: 5. Purposes
sections.body: Registration and providing an account; saving favorites, publishing comments and ratings; handling enquiries via the feedback form; sending informational materials with consent; ensuring the Site's operation and security; statistics and analytics.

sections.title: 6. Legal bases
sections.body: The data subject's consent; a contract to which the subject is a party (provision of the Site's services); the operator's legitimate interests and the requirements of the law of the Russian Federation.

sections.title: 7. Consent and withdrawal
sections.body: By providing data through the Site's forms, the subject consents to its processing under this Policy. Consent may be withdrawn at any time by writing to hello@baikal-guide.ru; after withdrawal the operator stops processing unless otherwise provided by law.

sections.title: 8. Procedure, conditions and terms
sections.body: Processing is carried out with and without automation tools. Data is stored no longer than required for the purposes or by law; once purposes are achieved or consent is withdrawn, data is deleted or anonymised.

sections.title: 9. Cross-border transfer
sections.body: Cross-border data transfer is carried out only in compliance with 152-FZ. If hosting or services are located outside Russia, the operator ensures adequate protection of subjects' rights.

sections.title: 10. Subject's rights
sections.body: The subject may obtain information about the processing of their data, request its clarification, blocking or destruction in case of incompleteness/inaccuracy or unlawful processing, withdraw consent, and appeal the operator's actions to Roskomnadzor or in court (Art. 14 of 152-FZ).

sections.title: 11. Protection measures
sections.body: The operator takes legal, organisational and technical measures to protect personal data from unlawful access, alteration, copying, distribution and destruction, in accordance with Art. 18.1 and 19 of 152-FZ.

sections.title: 12. Final provisions
sections.body: The operator may amend this Policy; a new version takes effect upon publication on this page. For personal-data matters, contact hello@baikal-guide.ru.
